title: Create requirements asset directory structure
notes: The repo now exposes a stable packet-facing requirements directory structure with canonical latest, catalogs, history, dashboards, and manifests entrypoints layered on top of the versioned imported packet.

title: Place v1.0 catalog assets as canonical sources
notes: The imported v1.0 packet assets are now available through stable repo-native canonical paths under requirements/latest, catalogs, history, dashboards, and manifests, while the versioned import tree remains the authoritative raw source bundle.

title: Define requirements CSV schema contract
notes: The shared packet loader and verification suite now validate the canonical packet CSV families beyond the master trio, including summary, delta, detailed requirement splits, C++ API catalog, and the imported API/MIM/XSD/WSDL catalogs, with required-column and row-shape checks wired into repo-native tests.

title: Add requirements loader module
notes: The repo now has a dedicated typed loader for the imported canonical packet bundle, including manifest access plus master, verification-matrix, and clause-tracker indexes by requirement_id, standard, clause, capability, feature, and test_id.

title: Add requirements lint command
notes: The repo now has a dedicated requirements lint command that centralizes imported packet manifest validation, canonical CSV schema checks, verification-matrix foreign-key checks, and clause-tracker coverage behind one supported interface.

title: Hook pytest tests to requirement IDs
notes: Representative witness tests across Clauses 4 through 10 now carry explicit pytest requirement markers, verification tests enforce the expected service-family coverage, and the reporting script can list covered requirement IDs from the annotated tests.

title: Add CI validation for requirements assets
notes: CI now runs the dedicated requirements lint command inside the required lint gate before implementation tests, so malformed imported requirements assets fail the standard CI path.

title: Generate human-readable requirements docs
notes: The repo now generates and ships packet-style markdown views directly from the canonical imported v1.0 master catalog, grouped by standard, clause, capability, and service group, with an explicit engineering-baseline caveat.

title: Map requirements to implementation modules
notes: The harmonized clause bridges now map packet-derived requirements to concrete implementation areas and repo-native tests across Clauses 5 through 11.

title: Peer-review source clause traceability
notes: The repo still treats the imported packet as an engineering baseline rather than a certified clause-by-clause legal trace, and no separate reviewed-status pass against the restricted standards inputs has been completed.

title: Create implementation work queues from requirements
notes: The repo now generates explicit requirement-driven work queues for Federation Management, Declaration Management, Object Management, Ownership, Time, DDM, Support, MOM/MIM, OMT, XML, and transports, with each queue item carrying requirement IDs and current acceptance-test anchors.

title: Decide handling of restricted standards inputs
notes: The repo explicitly preserves generated catalogs while excluding restricted IEEE source PDFs and zip artifacts from the committed import tree.
